title: dorstenia cuspidata var. humblotiana
intro: Dorstenia cuspidata var. humblotiana, also known as the Humblot's leatherfan, is a plant species within the family Moraceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Madagascar, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as relieving fever, reducing pain, and treating skin conditions, due to its high concentration of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and tannins. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
